steps.title: Identify the task
steps.content: The student wants to contrast the locations of the two specimens. The notes give two distinct locations: Cheryll is at the Palm Beach Museum of Natural History in Wellington, Florida, and Dio is at the Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada. The correct choice must place both locations side by side in a contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Cheryll is housed at the Palm Beach Museum of Natural History in Wellington, Florida, while Dio is housed at the Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada.: names both specific locations and uses "while" to directly contrast them. ✓
  • Choice B — The *Triceratops* fossil specimen Cheryll is not the only such specimen currently housed in a museum or institute.: mentions only Cheryll and omits any location for either specimen. ✗
  • Choice C — The Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada, houses Dio, a *Triceratops* fossil specimen.: gives Dio's location but omits Cheryll's location entirely, so no contrast is made. ✗
  • Choice D — Like Cheryll, Dio is a *Triceratops* fossil specimen that lived in the Late Cretaceous period.: emphasizes a similarity (shared time period) rather than a difference in location. ✗
That matches Choice A — Cheryll is housed at the Palm Beach Museum of Natural History in Wellington, Florida, while Dio is housed at the Royal Ontario Museum in Ontario, Canada..
